flags.concern: Resize overlap math: Step 4 computes the new video speed from the requested resize span instead of the overlap-resolved span, so a clamp inside `resolveOverlaps()` would leave the clip with the wrong playback rate.
flags.evidence: Revised plan addresses all 15 flags: (1) overlap speed formula now uses resolved span from resolvedRows, not requested span; (2) commits to post-processing overlap patches in useClipResize instead of modifying resolveOverlaps; (3) concrete data path via existing useTimelineEditorData() context which already has dataRef; (4) explicit dir destructuring fix and inline type instead of importing unexported ResizeDir; (5) test mock updated with dataRef; (6) visual cue specified concretely with resizeClamped state and ring class; (7) criteria re-prioritized — unautomatable ones demoted, redundant playbackRate criterion removed.

flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts:54-73` against revised Step 1. The plan now says to cache `to` in `resizeStartRef`, but it still never states whether that value must come from committed clip state or from a fresh `getSourceTime(...)` calculation. Because the current resize handler computes a transient wrong `to` during left-edge trims before commit-time rematerialization repairs it, leaving `origin.to` implicit is still a correctness-sensitive ambiguity.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resolve-overlaps.ts`. The helper can clamp the resized clip again after the requested drag by changing `start` and `end` before commit, so the plan's Step 4 formula must use the resolved clip span from `resolvedRows`, not the requested `action.end - action.start`; otherwise overlap-adjusted video clips will get the wrong playback speed.

flags.concern: Resize hook mutation: revised Step 2.4 clamps audio by mutating `action.end`, even though the current resize hook builds new row objects immutably from the callback input.
flags.evidence: In `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts:76-100`, the current code reads `action.start` and `action.end` and then creates replacement row actions; revised Step 2.4 instead proposes `action.end = ...`.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked revised Step 2.4 against `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts:76-100`. The plan still says to clamp audio by writing back to `action.end`, but the current hook treats the callback `action` as input data and then creates fresh row actions from it. Mutating that caller-owned object is unnecessary and risks coupling the resize hook to outside references instead of using a local `clampedEnd` variable.
